india_trends.title: Raja Venkatraman, MarketSmith recommend five stocks for 15 April
india_trends.summary: Domestic markets closed on April 14 due to Dr. Baba Saheb Ambedkar Jayanti. On April 15, the BSE Sensex fell by 0.91% while the NSE Nifty 50 decreased by 0.86%. Analysts suggest a strong market opening influenced by improving global cues despite rising oil prices.

india_trends.title: RBI rejects banks’ request to spread out Q4 treasury losses
india_trends.summary: The RBI has declined banks’ request to stagger provisions for likely MTM losses in the March quarter. Banks had sought relief to cushion the impact of rising bond yields and a late-imposed $100 million cap on net open positions, both of which pressured treasury earnings.

india_trends.title: Dollar and VIX Are Back in Tandem as Iran War Usurps Tariff Bets
india_trends.summary: The dollar’s traditional relationship with equity market volatility has been revived by the war in Iran, a signal that haven-seeking investors are finding safety in the US assets they snubbed after last year’s tariff turmoil.

india_trends.title: Crude oil prices extend losses for second day on US-Iran peace talks hopes; Brent oil slips below $95 a barrel
india_trends.summary: Crude oil prices fell for the second consecutive session as US-Iran peace talks may resume, easing supply concerns. Brent crude fell to $94.44, while US West Texas Intermediate dropped to $90.33.

india_trends.title: Stocks in news: ICICI Pru Life, Anand Rathi Share, LIC, Vedanta, HAL
india_trends.summary: Markets experienced volatility, declining nearly 1% due to weak global cues, with analysts emphasizing the critical support level of 23,500 for a rebound. Several companies like ICICI Pru Life, Anand Rathi Share, LIC, Vedanta, and HAL are in focus due to Q4 results and news developments.
